original_hypothesis.title: Mint and Burn Operations Bypass Pause Mechanism
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: mint() and burn() check only the _operating flag, not the paused() state. When ADMIN pauses the contract for emergency, minters can continue minting and burning tokens. This contradicts the expected behavior of pause() halting all value-movement operations and creates a supply manipulation vector during emergencies.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: For a regulated stablecoin, the pause mechanism is a critical emergency control. If minting continues during pause (intended to freeze all activity), an exploiting minter can create unbacked tokens or reduce supply while transfers are frozen, causing accounting discrepancies.

proof_of_signal.pattern: mint() checks 'if (!_operating) revert OperationsOff()' at line 133 but never checks paused(); burn() similarly at line 155

preconditions: ADMIN has paused the contract via pause()
A MINTER_ROLE or MASTER_MINTER holder calls mint() or burn()
_operating is still true (safety switch not engaged)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: paused() == true but mint(to, amount) succeeds because only _operating is checked
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Minter creates tokens while all transfers are frozen; when unpaused, excess supply enters circulation
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Minter mints tokens to own address during pause, sells immediately when unpaused

economic_questions: Is the separation between pause and _operating intentional?
What is the maximum damage during a pause window?
Can minted tokens be moved via forceTransfer during pause?
validation_requirements: Test: pause() then mint() - confirm mint succeeds
Test: pause() then burn() - confirm burn succeeds
Compare with documentation claiming pause halts operations

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Market liquidity needed post-unpause to sell
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Supply inflation during crisis exacerbates damage

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total minter allowances available during pause

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm mint/burn succeed when paused() is true

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
